I said, well, Leo, it doesn't make any sense. Because I kind of like it. It's all right. Fair enough. I'll just show you how to say it. So if you say it a certain way, no more of a question it. Now by using the presupposing tone. So he goes, I asked my clients, the line is going to be, I only asked my clients to judge me on my losers because I have so few. Well, what the fuck, what does that even mean? Right? It doesn't mean anything. Right? It's kind of a stupid statement. But because of the way he says it, the tonality he uses, you're just like, oh, yeah, of course. It's like you just buy into the idea. That's a presupposing tone. That's called your beyond obvious. Your implied obviousness. Okay? So I'm trying to explain how obvious the statement is. Like, how could you even agree with this? Is that I never asked my clients to judge me on my winners.
